title: Demo: Sunday Night Dread — Voice-to-Invoice MVP
problem: A local plumber, electrician, or carpenter is brilliant at their craft but spends every Sunday evening hunched over a laptop, reconstructing material costs, labor hours, and invoices from memory and coffee-stained scraps of paper collected throughout the week. This administrative tax steals their rest and leads to forgotten billables. The MVP slice: while driving between jobs, the tradesperson speaks voice notes to their dashboard. The system parses material and labor mentions and emits a draft invoice in their inbox by Sunday morning. Project versioning, inventory linking, and full as-built documentation are explicitly OUT of scope for the MVP — those become the "next iterations" roadmap on the showcase page. Scoped as Tier 2 (~$1500). This is simultaneously the cornerstone demo (replacing the discovery-call CTA with a scrollable replica of the actual experience), the first published showcase, the projectability anchor for the Marie/Lucie persona archetypes from the brainstorming session, and a genuine solve for a real Quebec tradesperson pain.
analysis: The cornerstone artifact of the entire portal — first concrete demo project that demonstrates the full engagement experience to visitors. Sunday Night Dread targets a real Quebec-tradesperson pain: voice-to-invoice MVP slice. The tradesperson speaks voice notes from their truck during the day; an AI parses material and labor mentions; a draft invoice lands in their inbox by Sunday morning. Built as a real working app — not screenshots — so visitors can poke at it. Simultaneously: (1) the discovery-call replacement (visitors see the entire engagement scrolling under their thumb), (2) the first showcase in the portfolio, (3) the projectability anchor ('I see myself here' for Marie/Lucie persona archetypes from Phase 2), (4) a real solve to a real Quebec pain. Scoped to MVP (voice → draft invoice). Project versioning, inventory, full as-built documentation become the 'next iterations' on the showcase page.

decisions.decision: Build the MVP slice (voice → draft invoice only), NOT the full vision
decisions.rationale: Insight #33 — the demo session anchors visible price expectations. Showing Tier 3 ambition undermines Tier 1-2 pricing. Story stays vivid; deliverable scope shrinks honestly.
decisions.recommendation: Voice capture + parse + invoice draft only. Full project versioning, inventory, version-controlled docs become 'next iterations roadmap' on the showcase page.

decisions.decision: Composite client voice ('based on real coordination problems from 3 tradespeople')
decisions.rationale: Phase 2 Q3 — labeled-fake undermines the demo; named real client requires consent and privacy work; composite is the honest middle path. Says 'illustrative, drawn from real conversations' without inventing a specific person.
decisions.recommendation: Composite framing in a small disclosure note on the showcase. Avoid scare-quotes or 'demo' watermarks — the work is real, only the client-attribution is composed.

decisions.decision: Hybrid build: real working app + portal artifacts visible together
decisions.rationale: Phase 2 Q2 — visitors see the entire arc (form, triage, plan, status flags, deliverable, showcase) by scrolling, AND can click to test the live working invoice generator.
decisions.recommendation: Embed real engagement-page replica showing all stages chronologically. Live preview iframe links to the actually-deployed voice-to-invoice MVP.

decisions.decision: Demo ships BEFORE first cold Facebook post
decisions.rationale: Phase 2 Q5 — the post links to the page; the page without the demo is dead. The demo IS the entire pitch.
decisions.recommendation: Demo deadline: 2026-07-01. External accountability: Marc tells his wife or a trusted friend the deadline (per Phase 5 push-back on F4).

successCriteria:
- Real working voice-to-draft-invoice app deployed and interactive from a live preview iframe
- Engagement page replica showing all 6 stages of the engagement (intake / triage / plan / build / ship / showcase) scrollable in 3-5 minutes
- Composite client voice with disclosure note (not labeled-fake, not real-named)
- Showcase page tagged Tier 2 with concrete metadata (price, hours, ship date)
- Demo live and tested before first Facebook cold post (deadline 2026-07-01)
- Story stays Tier 2-scoped; full vision becomes 'next iterations' roadmap on the showcase

statusHistory.reason: Created during brainstorming session — feat-2026-010 spec'd as the cornerstone demo (Tier 2, voice→draft invoice MVP).

Adding real audio playback to the 3 fixture clips. Marc to record himself reading existing transcripts as scrappy voice memos and drop them at portal/public/audio/. Rationale: the play button currently reveals text only — real audio (with truck/garage background noise) is the one thing the static showcase cannot fake, and it strengthens the cornerstone artifact's authenticity. Composite-voice disclosure already covers attribution. On-device-Whisper + email-delivery decisions remain valid for the production tradesperson app, still out of scope for the showcase.

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Initial fill from brainstorming — content captures Phase 2 Demo Session decisions (Q1 problem, Q2 hybrid build, Q3 composite voice, Q5 ship-before-post).

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Locked scope: MVP slice is voice→draft invoice ONLY. Project versioning, inventory, as-built docs, accounting export explicitly held back as 'next iterations.' Driven by Insight #33 — the demo session anchors visible price expectations; showing Tier 3 ambition undermines Tier 1-2 pricing.

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Composite client voice approach locked (Phase 2 Q3): drawn from real conversations with 3 tradespeople, never named. Avoids both labeled-fake (undermines demo) and named-real (privacy work). Disclosure note appears on showcase page.

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Hybrid build approach locked (Phase 2 Q2): real working app + portal artifacts visible together. Visitors see the entire engagement arc by scrolling AND can click into a real invoice generator. Single most important UX decision for the cornerstone demo.

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Ship sequencing locked (Phase 2 Q5): demo MUST go live before first cold Facebook post. The post links to the page; the page without the demo is dead. Deadline: 2026-07-01. External accountability: Marc tells wife or trusted friend per Phase 5 push-back on F4.

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Added schema-conformant showcase annotation: bilingual title/summary/disclosure, 6-stage engagement timeline, Tier 2 metadata, 4 engineering decisions about voice→invoice approach (email delivery, on-device processing, free-text + lexicon, bilingual mid-note). Renderer reads from data field.

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Added lineage with parent (feat-2026-001) and 4 children scaffolded as next-iteration features (feat-2026-011 versioning, 012 inventory, 013 as-built, 014 accounting). Showcase page now renders these from the file tree — adding a future iteration = adding a folder.

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Sync feature.json to shipped reality: SndDemo.tsx + sndParser.ts already deployed as a static interactive showcase. Documenting the deliberate choice to keep the showcase static (fixture clips, rendered email, no backend) — the on-device-voice + real-email decisions remain valid for the future production tradesperson app, but are out of scope for the showcase demo. Adds the four fields needed to advance to frozen.

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Wired real audio playback into the 3 fixture clips. Added optional audioSrc field to VoiceClip, pointing to /audio/snd-{id}.mp3. SndDemo.tsx now renders an <audio preload="none"> per clip and triggers .play()/.pause() in togglePlay. Graceful degradation: missing-file rejection is swallowed, transcript still reveals. Marc to record himself reading the existing transcripts as scrappy phone voice memos with ambient truck/garage noise; composite-voice disclosure covers attribution. Strengthens the cornerstone artifact's authenticity — the play button now does what visitors expect.

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Auto-stamp pipeline test 1/3: prepended a 🎯 emoji to the SND disclaimer string (FR + EN). This revision should be the first one auto-stamped by the deploy.yml workflow with `commit` (the SHA of this push) and `buildUrl` (the wrangler-action deployment URL). Visitors clicking 'voir ce build →' on this entry should land on a build whose disclaimer reads '🎯 Démo statique...'.

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Auto-stamp pipeline test 2/3: swapped the disclaimer emoji 🎯 → 🔥 to verify a second sequential auto-stamp produces a distinct buildUrl. Visitors comparing the test 1 and test 2 iframes side-by-side should see one 🎯 and one 🔥 in the disclaimer line at the bottom of each demo.

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Auto-stamp pipeline test 3/3: removed the disclaimer emoji entirely (back to plain text). With this revision stamped, the build log shows three sequential iframes — 🎯, 🔥, none — that visitors can click through to compare actual rendered builds. Confirms the time-travel pipeline works end-to-end.

annotations.author: marc+claude
annotations.body: Public showcase metadata: tier, price, hours, engagement timeline, and bilingual narrative. Read by the Showcase page renderer in the portal app.

title.fr: Sunday Night Dread — voix → brouillon de facture
title.en: Sunday Night Dread — voice → draft invoice

summary.fr: Plombier-électricien-menuisier qui passe son dimanche soir à reconstruire les heures et les matériaux de la semaine. Notes vocales depuis le truck → brouillon de facture le dimanche matin.
summary.en: Plumber-electrician-carpenter spending Sunday nights reconstructing hours and materials. Voice notes from the truck → draft invoice by Sunday morning.

compositeDisclosure.fr: Voix client composite — basée sur des conversations réelles avec 3 gens de métier. Le code et l'app sont vrais et déployés.
compositeDisclosure.en: Composite client voice — drawn from real conversations with 3 tradespeople. The code and the app are real and deployed.

label.fr: Réception du formulaire — 3 voix de gens de métier composées
Triage accepté en 48 h — projet scopé Tier 2
Plan figé : MVP voix → brouillon de facture seulement
label.en: Plan locked: MVP voice → draft invoice only

label.fr: En construction — démo testable en cours
Revue avec le client (composite) — 1 ronde de retouches
label.en: Review with the (composite) client — 1 round of tweaks

decision.fr: Livraison par email, pas dans une nouvelle app
decision.en: Email delivery, not yet another app

rationale.fr: Le tradesperson ouvre déjà sa boîte de réception le dimanche matin. Ajouter une app à apprendre = friction. Le brouillon de facture arrive là où il regarde déjà.
rationale.en: The tradesperson already opens their inbox Sunday morning. Adding another app to learn = friction. The draft invoice lands where they're already looking.

decision.fr: Notes vocales traitées sur l'appareil — seul le texte parsé monte au cloud
decision.en: Voice notes processed on-device — only the parsed text uploads

rationale.fr: Confidentialité : les clips vocaux contiennent souvent des noms de clients et des prix. Ils ne quittent jamais le téléphone. Le compromis : le téléphone doit pouvoir faire tourner le modèle de transcription (iPhone ≥12, Android récent).
rationale.en: Privacy: voice clips often include client names and prices. They never leave the phone. Trade-off: the phone needs to run the transcription model (iPhone ≥12, recent Android).

decision.fr: Texte libre + lexique de chantier québécois plutôt que listes déroulantes
decision.en: Free text + Quebec construction lexicon rather than dropdown menus

rationale.fr: « Deux 2x4 et un seau de plâtre » se parle naturellement; choisir « plâtre » dans une liste de 200 items est exaspérant. Le lexique est curé à la main pour le vocabulaire des métiers québécois et grandit selon les corrections.
rationale.en: “Two 2x4s and a bucket of plaster” is what gets said naturally; picking “plaster” from a 200-item dropdown is infuriating. The lexicon is hand-curated for Quebec trades vocabulary and grows from corrections.

decision.fr: Voix bilingue FR + EN dans une seule boîte de réception
decision.en: Bilingual voice FR + EN in a single inbox

rationale.fr: Sur les chantiers québécois, la langue switch souvent à l'intérieur d'une même note (« faut commander deux sheets of drywall »). Forcer un choix de langue à l'avance casse l'écoulement naturel.
rationale.en: On Quebec job sites, language switches mid-note ("faut commander two sheets of drywall"). Forcing a language pick up-front breaks the natural flow.

annotations.author: lac advance
annotations.body: Adding real audio playback to the 3 fixture clips. Marc to record himself reading existing transcripts as scrappy voice memos and drop them at portal/public/audio/. Rationale: the play button currently reveals text only — real audio (with truck/garage background noise) is the one thing the static showcase cannot fake, and it strengthens the cornerstone artifact's authenticity. Composite-voice disclosure already covers attribution. On-device-Whisper + email-delivery decisions remain valid for the production tradesperson app, still out of scope for the showcase.

implementation: Static-only interactive showcase deployed at /demo/sunday-night-dread (FR) and /en/demo/sunday-night-dread (EN). Three pre-recorded fixture voice clips with hand-typed bilingual transcripts live as VOICE_CLIPS in src/lib/sndParser.ts. Each clip carries an optional audioSrc pointing to /audio/snd-{id}.mp3 — a real voice recording (Marc reading the transcript with ambient truck/garage noise) that adds the one texture the static showcase cannot fake. Audio elements use preload="none" so files only download on first play; .play() rejection on missing-file or autoplay policy is swallowed so the demo degrades gracefully to text-only. Visitor clicks the play button on a clip; the audio plays and the transcript reveals simultaneously. The transcript flows through parseTranscript() — a regex + curated Quebec-trades lexicon parser that extracts material mentions (with quantity + unit) and labor hours. After at least one clip is played, a Generate button calls buildInvoice() to render an email-shaped invoice card per client: a labor line at the standard $75/h rate × hours, material lines priced from the lexicon, TPS 5%, TVQ 9.975%, total. Bilingual rendering via the existing i18n.ts dictionary keyed off the lang prop (route-driven). No backend, no API, no real-time recording in the browser, no actual email delivery — the entire experience runs in the browser from static fixtures + bundled audio assets. The demo deliberately avoids mic permission prompts and model downloads to keep visitor flow frictionless. The on-device Whisper + email-delivery decisions in the brainstorming spec apply to the future production tradesperson app, not the showcase. Component: src/pages/SndDemo.tsx + src/lib/sndParser.ts (parser + invoice math + fixtures) + public/audio/ (bundled MP3 voiceovers).
userGuide: On the Sunday Night Dread page, three voice notes from a tradesperson's Friday afternoon are listed — each from a different job site. Click the play button on any clip to see the transcript. As clips play, a parsed panel appears showing what the system extracts: hours, job type, materials with quantity and unit. After at least one clip is played, click Génère/Generate to build the email-shaped invoice draft — one per client, with TPS/TVQ taxes and a labor line at the standard rate. Toggle clips on and off and the invoice rebuilds automatically. The whole thing runs in your browser — no recording, no upload, no account.
knownLimitations: Static fixtures only — no live in-browser voice recording. Visitors hear pre-recorded MP3s of Marc reading the transcripts; real on-device voice capture is intentionally deferred to the production tradesperson app, not the showcase demo.
Audio is composite-voice (Marc reading the scripted transcripts), not the actual tradespeople from the brainstorming. The composite-voice disclosure on the showcase covers attribution.
Email is rendered as HTML in the browser, not actually delivered. The visual artifact communicates the experience without requiring a backend or email service.

Parser only recognizes a small curated Quebec-trades lexicon (drywall, 2x4, plâtre, vis, etc.) — unfamiliar items pass through but are not priced. Lexicon grows by hand-curated additions, not auto-learned.
No persistence — toggle state and generated invoices reset on page refresh. The static showcase has no need for user accounts or storage.
Requires JavaScript. The showcase is rendered client-side from React; a static-HTML fallback is not implemented (acceptable for a portfolio demo).

Bilingual transcripts are hand-translated FR/EN pairs in the fixture file. Audio is recorded once per clip in the language Marc records in (typically FR with mid-note EN code-switching, per the bilingual-voice decision); the transcripts UI mirror is what switches with route language, not the audio.
Labor rate is fixed at $75/h in the parser; per-tradesperson rate configuration is out of scope for the showcase.
